summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on three variance cases. Cases BZA 2025-020 and 2025-021, continued from October 20, involved Rachel and John Walker's request for variances to operate an agritourism/vendor market business on two R2-zoned parcels on Kerbet Road, including a 6,240 square foot barn for a farmer's market and allowance for secondary vendors and food trucks. Case BZA 2025-022 involved a request for a variance to build a 6,000 square foot aircraft hangar on a property adjacent to a private airstrip on Huntley Road. All three variances were approved unanimously 4-0.
decisions: Approved BZA 2025-020 and 2025-021 variances for agritourism/farmer's market operation on Kerbet Road with no stipulation for paved entrance parking (4-0)
Approved BZA 2025-022 variance for 6,000 square foot aircraft hangar on Huntley Road (4-0)
action_items: Staff to publish findings of fact for BZA 2025-020/021 and begin permit process for the Walkers
Staff to publish findings of fact for BZA 2025-022 and begin permit process for the hangar
Staff to send email to solidify next year's BZA meeting dates if no new cases are filed by December 2nd deadline
key_amounts: 6,240 square foot barn proposed for farmer's market (BZA 2025-020/021)
800 square foot maximum allowed for farmer's market use without variance
Maximum 200 vendors/food trucks combined for vendor market events
5-acre parking area proposed for agritourism property
4,000 square foot hangar to be built initially with expansion to 6,000 square feet (BZA 2025-022)
2,000 square foot maximum for accessory structures per current zoning code
Events limited to no more than 2 times per month, May through November
notable_topics: Agritourism vs commercial use determination for farmer's market/vendor operation
Parking surface material debate - crushed limestone chips vs paved surface requirements
Dust mitigation concerns for gravel driveway and parking
Traffic control on Kerbet Road for vendor market events
Aircraft hangar variance for private airstrip property on Huntley Road
Tree preservation along Huntley Road for hangar property
School bus turnaround and road vacation history on Huntley Road
2021 zoning resolution change adding 2,000 sq ft aggregate limit for accessory structures
attendees: John Grooms (Public - 1871 Kerbet Road, in support)
Elvin Miller (Applicant representative - 1011 Blue Sky Drive, BZA 2025-022)
Adam Palmer (Public - 1727 East Huntley Road, in support)
Caleb Palmer (Public - 1727 East Huntley Road, in support)
Sean Becker/Pefrer (Property owner - BZA 2025-022, not present but referenced)
notes: Mr. Shaw was absent. Cases BZA 2025-020 and 2025-021 were continued from the October 20, 2025 meeting. Beverly Welburn made an unusual statement about feeling harmed by EMFs/microwaves in the meeting building. The Walkers' property is on a dead-end street next to ball fields on Kerbet Road. The hangar property is part of a private air park redeveloped by Elvin Miller in the early 2000s. The property owner (Becker/Pefrer) is a retired NKU accounting professor living in northern Kentucky who plans to build the hangar first, sell his Kentucky home, then build the house. Staff's recommendation for 50-200 feet of paved entrance was not adopted as a stipulation. Next meeting scheduled for December 22, 2025. Meeting adjourned at 7:42 PM.
title: BZA-11-17-2025
